items.h: Open https://michaelnocito.github.io/playtest-tracker/ with the console open (F12). No red console errors, no blank screen.
Pick any game or kit from the sidebar. Its 6-pass suite (or 🎯 Sprint block) should render with no blank panel.
Cycle one checklist item through pass/fail/blocked, add a note, then check the app again after a moment — it should still be there (Supabase Realtime).
Type something in the always-on quick-capture bar, tag it, hit Add. It should appear in the Feedback & Roadmap inbox as Untriaged.
After marking a fail, the 'Next:' banner should reflect it's Claude's turn. After Claude marks a result 'fixed', it should say it's your turn to retest.
Click each summary pill (pass/fail/blocked/untested/retest) — the list should filter to just that status.
Reload the page. Runs, results, and inbox items should all still be there (pulled fresh from Supabase).
Shrink the window narrow and flip to light mode. Layout should hold, text readable, nothing cut off.

suite.pass: 6. Did a fix break anything? — after fixing bugs

items.h: Re-check everything near what you changed, not only the one bug you were chasing.

items.t: Re-ran the smoke + happy path passes
items.h: Do passes 1–2 again fully. A fix that breaks the tracker itself is worse than the bug it fixed.

debrief.q2: History reconstructed from git log, not a live playtest run — see inbox for what's still open.

inbox.text: The tracker itself is now a tracked app (like every game/kit) — future feature requests, bugs, and ideas for the tracker go through this inbox instead of ad-hoc chat notes.

actions.note: Self-referential onboarding: adding playtest-tracker as an entry in its own tool.
